# LinkedIn Draft - 2026-06-25

The release adds AbortSignal-based cancellation plus a normalized cancellation error, and removes CommonJS entrypoints in favor of ESM-only packages on Node 22.22.3+.

Went through 40 links today. Here's what made the brief:

1. Composio core adds cancellation and drops CJS support
   Cancellation is directly useful for agent reliability, and the ESM-only/Node 22 change is the kind of dependency break that can silently bite automation stacks.

2. Mistral adds tighter connector controls for agents
   This is practical agent-ops work: identity, tool scoping, and failure debugging are the pieces that usually break production connector setups.

3. Free tiers keep attracting abuse pressure
   Recurring solo-founder trap: a free tier is not just pricing, it is an abuse surface that can consume product time.

4. One founder’s year-one SaaS lessons are mundane but useful
   Most builder retrospectives are vague; this one at least names concrete cost and payment decisions that matter early.

5. Three paying users is small, but the measurement discipline is right
   The numbers are tiny, but the measurement mindset is the right one and worth stealing if you are early-stage.

Full brief with all 40 researched links → https://thenightlylibrarian.com/briefs/2026-06-25

Draft status: not approved for posting.
